field_reference.overview: Consumed by bmc_init_sysfs_gpio() in hw-management-bmc-gpio-set.sh after hw-management-bmc-plat-specific-preps copies the platform file to /etc/hw-management-bmc-gpio-pins.json (or override GPIO_PINS_CONFIG). Parsed with hw-management-bmc-json-parser.sh.
field_reference.description: string, optional. Comment only; not required by the script.

bmc_stby_ready.chip: string, required. Logical GPIO controller: aspeed | expander24 (see chip table).
bmc_stby_ready.offset: number, required. Line index relative to that controller base (Linux GPIO number = discovered_base + offset).

field_reference.pins: array of pin objects, processed in order (export, direction, optional value, then after wait_platform_drv symlinks).

pin_object.chip: string, required. aspeed — SoC GPIO (AST2600 208 lines or AST2700 216 lines, auto-detected). expander24 — 24-line PCAL6524-class expander.
pin_object.offset: number, required. Offset on that chip.
pin_object.value: number, optional. 0 or 1; applied only when direction is out (omit for direction-only setup).
pin_object.symlink: string, optional. If set and /var/run/hw-management/system exists, check_n_link gpio<abs>/value -> <system_path>/<symlink>.

runtime.BMC_STBY_READY: Global path set from bmc_stby_ready chip+offset for wait_bmc_standby_ready() in hw-management-bmc-ready.sh.

field_reference.deployment_note: Do not install this whole file to /etc. Copy only the object in example_platform below (save as /etc/hw-management-bmc-gpio-pins.json), or use the platform tree file usr/etc/<HID>/hw-management-bmc-gpio-pins.json shipped in the package. Remove field_reference and deployment_note from any deployed JSON.

example_platform.description: HI189 / SPC6 AST2700 reference — same logical content as usr/etc/HI189/hw-management-bmc-gpio-pins.json in this repo.
